From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American television writer and producer

| Jeff Pinkner | |
| --- | --- |
| Pinkner at [San Diego Comic-Con](/wiki/San_Diego_Comic-Con "San Diego Comic-Con") in July 2010. | |
| Born | November 16, 1964 (1964-11-16) (age 61) |
| Other names | Jeffrey Pinkner |
| Occupations | Writer, producer |
| Years active | 1996–present |

**Jeff Pinkner** (born November 16, 1964) is an American television and movie writer and producer.

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jeff_Pinkner&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Born to a [Jewish](/wiki/American_Jews "American Jews") family,[[1]](#cite_note-VarietyFamily-1) Pinkner graduated from [Pikesville High School](/wiki/Pikesville_High_School "Pikesville High School") in [Baltimore, Maryland](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore") in 1983, [Northwestern University](/wiki/Northwestern_University "Northwestern University") in 1987, and [Harvard Law School](/wiki/Harvard_Law_School "Harvard Law School") in 1990.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jeff_Pinkner&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

He is known for his work on *[Alias](/wiki/Alias_(TV_series) "Alias (TV series)")* where he served as executive producer. In 2006 and 2007, he worked as an executive producer and writer for the [mystery](/wiki/Mystery_fiction "Mystery fiction") series *[Lost](/wiki/Lost_(2004_TV_series) "Lost (2004 TV series)")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The *Lost* writing staff, including Pinkner, were nominated for the [Writers Guild of America (WGA) Award](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Award "Writers Guild of America Award") for Best Dramatic Series at the [February 2007 ceremony](/wiki/Writers_Guild_of_America_Awards_2006 "Writers Guild of America Awards 2006") for their work on the second and third seasons of *Lost*.[[3]](#cite_note-WGA07-3) In 2010, he has an overall deal with Warner Bros. TV.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

Pinkner wrote [Columbia Pictures](/wiki/Columbia_Pictures "Columbia Pictures")'s *[The Amazing Spider-Man 2](/wiki/The_Amazing_Spider-Man_2 "The Amazing Spider-Man 2")* script with [Roberto Orci](/wiki/Roberto_Orci "Roberto Orci") and [Alex Kurtzman](/wiki/Alex_Kurtzman "Alex Kurtzman"). The film starred [Andrew Garfield](/wiki/Andrew_Garfield "Andrew Garfield") and [Emma Stone](/wiki/Emma_Stone "Emma Stone"), and was directed by [Marc Webb](/wiki/Marc_Webb "Marc Webb"). It opened in the U.S. May 2014.[[5]](#cite_note-spiderhire-5)

He frequently collaborates with a tightly knit group of film professionals which include [J. J. Abrams](/wiki/J._J._Abrams "J. J. Abrams"), [Damon Lindelof](/wiki/Damon_Lindelof "Damon Lindelof"), [Adam Horowitz](/wiki/Adam_Horowitz "Adam Horowitz"), [Alex Kurtzman](/wiki/Alex_Kurtzman "Alex Kurtzman"), [Roberto Orci](/wiki/Roberto_Orci "Roberto Orci"), [Edward Kitsis](/wiki/Edward_Kitsis "Edward Kitsis"), [Andre Nemec](/wiki/Andre_Nemec "Andre Nemec"), [Josh Appelbaum](/wiki/Josh_Appelbaum "Josh Appelbaum"), and [Bryan Burk](/wiki/Bryan_Burk "Bryan Burk").[[1]](#cite_note-VarietyFamily-1)

### *Fringe*

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jeff_Pinkner&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Fringe")]

In 2008, Pinkner began developing the [FOX](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Network "Fox Broadcasting Network") [science fiction](/wiki/Science_fiction "Science fiction") series *[Fringe](/wiki/Fringe_(TV_series) "Fringe (TV series)")*, along with co-creators [Alex Kurtzman](/wiki/Alex_Kurtzman "Alex Kurtzman"), [J. J. Abrams](/wiki/J._J._Abrams "J. J. Abrams") and [Roberto Orci](/wiki/Roberto_Orci "Roberto Orci"). Pinkner served as co-showrunner, executive producer, and writer (titles he shared with [J. H. Wyman](/wiki/J._H._Wyman "J. H. Wyman")) through the show's [fourth season](/wiki/Fringe_(season_4) "Fringe (season 4)").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) After the conclusion of season four, Pinkner left the series.[[8]](#cite_note-8) Episodes he contributed to include:

* "**[The Same Old Story](/wiki/The_Same_Old_Story "The Same Old Story")**" (01.02) (co-written by Orci, Abrams, and Kurtzman)
* "**[The Arrival](/wiki/The_Arrival_(Fringe) "The Arrival (Fringe)")**" (01.04) (co-written by Abrams)
* "**[In Which We Meet Mr. Jones](/wiki/In_Which_We_Meet_Mr._Jones "In Which We Meet Mr. Jones")**" (01.07) (co-written with Abrams)
* "**[Bound](/wiki/Bound_(Fringe) "Bound (Fringe)")**" (02.11) (co-written by Abrams, Orci, and Kurtzman)
* "**[The Road Not Taken](/wiki/The_Road_Not_Taken_(Fringe) "The Road Not Taken (Fringe)")**" (01.19) (Pinkner and supervising producer [J. R. Orci](/wiki/J._R._Orci "J. R. Orci") co-wrote the teleplay, based on a story by consulting producer [Akiva Goldsman](/wiki/Akiva_Goldsman "Akiva Goldsman"))
* "**[There's More Than One of Everything](/wiki/There%27s_More_Than_One_of_Everything "There's More Than One of Everything")**" (01.20) (Pinkner and Wyman co-wrote the teleplay, based on a story by Goldsman and executive producer [Bryan Burk](/wiki/Bryan_Burk "Bryan Burk"))
* "**[Night of Desirable Objects](/wiki/Night_of_Desirable_Objects_(Fringe) "Night of Desirable Objects (Fringe)")**" (02.02) (co-written with Wyman)
* "**[August](/wiki/August_(Fringe_episode) "August (Fringe episode)")**" (02.08) (co-written by Wyman)
* "**[Peter](/wiki/Peter_(Fringe_episode) "Peter (Fringe episode)")**" (02.16) (Pinkner, Wyman, and supervising producer [Josh Singer](/wiki/Josh_Singer "Josh Singer") wrote the teleplay based on a story along with Goldsman)
* "**[Brown Betty](/wiki/Brown_Betty_(Fringe) "Brown Betty (Fringe)")**" (02.20) (co-written with Goldsman and Wyman)
* "**[Over There (Part 1)](/wiki/Over_There_(Fringe) "Over There (Fringe)")**" (02.22) (co-written by Wyman and Goldsman)
* "**[Over There (Part 2)](/wiki/Over_There_(Fringe) "Over There (Fringe)")**" (02.23) (co-written by Goldsman and Wyman)
* "**[Olivia](/wiki/Olivia_(Fringe_episode) "Olivia (Fringe episode)")**" (03.01) (co-written with Wyman)
* "**[Entrada](/wiki/Entrada_(Fringe) "Entrada (Fringe)")**" (03.08) (co-written by Wyman)
* "**[The Firefly](/wiki/The_Firefly_(Fringe) "The Firefly (Fringe)")**" (03.10) (co-written with Wyman)
* "**[Subject 13](/wiki/Subject_13_(Fringe) "Subject 13 (Fringe)")**" (03.15) (co-written with Goldsman and Wyman)
* "**[Stowaway](/wiki/Stowaway_(Fringe) "Stowaway (Fringe)")**" (03.17) (Danielle Dispaltro wrote the teleplay, based on a story from Pinkner, Wyman, and Goldsman)
* "**[Lysergic Acid Diethylamide](/wiki/Lysergic_Acid_Diethylamide_(Fringe) "Lysergic Acid Diethylamide (Fringe)")**" (03.19) (Pinkner and Wyman wrote a teleplay based on a story along with Goldsman)
* "**[The Day We Died](/wiki/The_Day_We_Died "The Day We Died")**" (03.22) (Pinkner and Wyman wrote a teleplay based on a story along with Goldsman)
* "**[Neither Here Nor There](/wiki/Neither_Here_Nor_There_(Fringe) "Neither Here Nor There (Fringe)")**" (04.01) (Pinkner and Wyman wrote a teleplay based on a story along with Goldsman)
* "**[Subject 9](/wiki/Subject_9 "Subject 9")**" (04.04) (co-written with Goldsman and Wyman)
* "**[Making Angels](/wiki/Making_Angels "Making Angels")**" (04.11) (co-written by Wyman and Goldsman)
* "**[Nothing as It Seems](/wiki/Nothing_as_It_Seems_(Fringe) "Nothing as It Seems (Fringe)")**" (04.16) (co-written with Goldsman)
* "**[Letters of Transit](/wiki/Letters_of_Transit "Letters of Transit")**" (04.19) (co-written by Goldsman and Wyman)
* "**[Brave New World (Part 1)](/wiki/Brave_New_World_(Fringe) "Brave New World (Fringe)")**" (04.21) (co-written by Wyman and Goldsman)
* "**[Brave New World (Part 2)](/wiki/Brave_New_World_(Fringe) "Brave New World (Fringe)")**" (04.22) (co-written by Wyman and Goldsman)

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jeff_Pinkner&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Filmography")]

**Film writer**

* *[The Amazing Spider-Man 2](/wiki/The_Amazing_Spider-Man_2 "The Amazing Spider-Man 2")* (2014)
* *[The 5th Wave](/wiki/The_5th_Wave_(film) "The 5th Wave (film)")* (2016)
* *[The Dark Tower](/wiki/The_Dark_Tower_(2017_film) "The Dark Tower (2017 film)")* (2017)
* *[Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle](/wiki/Jumanji:_Welcome_to_the_Jungle "Jumanji: Welcome to the Jungle")* (2017)
* *[Venom](/wiki/Venom_(2018_film) "Venom (2018 film)")* (2018)
* *[Jumanji: The Next Level](/wiki/Jumanji:_The_Next_Level "Jumanji: The Next Level")* (2019)
* [Untitled *Jumanji: The Next Level* sequel](/wiki/Untitled_Jumanji:_The_Next_Level_sequel "Untitled Jumanji: The Next Level sequel") (2026)

## External links

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Jeff_Pinkner&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: External links")]

[![logo](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/en/thumb/4/4a/Commons-logo.svg/40px-Commons-logo.svg.png)](/wiki/File:Commons-logo.svg)
